items.title: APAC FX: Intervention and vulnerability to USD – BNY
items.summary: BNY’s Bob Savage focuses on rising FX intervention and fragile sentiment in Asia-Pacific (APAC) as higher Oil and geopolitical risks pressure regional currencies.

items.title: CAD: Jobs data and geopolitics steer rates – TD Securities
items.summary: TD Securities strategists Robert Both and Emma Lawrence highlight that Canadian rates are opening weaker, with yields tracking US moves and geopolitical tensions. They expect CAD employment to show only a modest rebound and see imported volatility dominating.

items.title: Brent: Conflict keeps risk premium elevated – OCBC
items.summary: OCBC strategists Sim Moh Siong and Christopher Wong argue that market hopes for de-escalation in the US–Iran conflict look premature for Brent.

items.title: When is the US ISM Services PMI data for March and how could it affect EUR/USD?
items.summary: The United States (US) Institute of Supply Management (ISM) Services Purchasing Managers’ Index (PMI) data for March is scheduled to be published today at 14:00 GMT.

items.title: WTI Oil drifts below $102.00 amid hopes of a peace deal in Iran
items.summary: US benchmark West Texas Intermediate Oil (WTI) depreciated about $5 from session highs on Monday, hitting lows near $101.00 per barrel, from $106.44 highs, weighed by news that the US and Iran might be bringing positions closer to a peace deal through international intermediaries.

items.title: USD: Oil-driven support reshapes path – OCBC
items.summary: OCBC strategists Sim Moh Siong and Christopher Wong highlight that higher Oil prices and safe-haven flows have re-anchored the Dollar, reversing expectations for a steady USD decline.

items.title: Canadian Dollar strengthens as US-Iran ceasefire talks weigh on US Dollar
items.summary: USD/CAD depreciates as the US Dollar (USD) eases amid improved market sentiment following the reports, suggesting prospects for a ceasefire in the Middle East. The pair trades around 1.3920 during the European hours on Monday.

items.title: USD: Escalation risks support Dollar strength – MUFG
items.summary: MUFG’s Lloyd Chan notes that persistent geopolitical tensions around Iran are reinforcing USD strength. Elevated US yields, resilient labour data and fading expectations for Fed rate cuts underpin Dollar carry appeal.

items.title: Oil: Supply shock drives global divergence – BNY
items.summary: BNY’s Bob Savage highlights a severe Oil supply shock, with front‑month Brent spiking above $140 and forward prices far lower, tightening global financial conditions.

items.title: Iran acknowledges receiving US ceasefire proposal through Pakistan - Reuters
items.summary: Iran has confirmed that it has received the proposal of a ceasefire by the United States (US) through Pakistan and has stated that it is being reviewed, according to a Senior Iranian official, Reuters reports.

items.title: US, Iran discuss 45-day ceasefire as mediators push for deal ahead of deadline
items.summary: The United States (US), Iran and regional mediators are discussing terms for a possible 45-day ceasefire that could lead to an end of fighting, Bloomberg reported on Monday, citing Axios. The people, who were not named, said chances of reaching a deal over the next 48 hours are low.

items.title: Asian stocks trade mixed amid Trump's fresh deadline to reopen the Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Asian equity markets opened mixed at the start of a new week as traders remain on edge amid the risk of a further escalation of tensions in the Middle East.
